title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Test 3 (Cambridge CPE 3)
source: Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English 3, Cambridge University Press

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-18, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 1: Cloze Texts

Farming Made Easy

Only a few years ago, crop farmers were fighting the cruel hand of fate with their backs firmly against the wall. Overseas production had driven down demand, resulting in (1) ______ bottom prices. As if that wasn't bad enough, a host of pesticide-resistant insects were eating (2) ______ way through the crops, and growers were almost powerless to stop them. Today, even if things aren't as (3) ______ as growers would like them to be, pest control isn't nearly the headache it was a decade ago. With the (4) ______ of a whole new family of insecticides, the job is likely to get even easier. We've got the best tools to control insects that we have ever had in the (5) ______ history of synthetic chemicals. But for now the issue is money. The new chemistry is very expensive and, unless many farmers haven't had an incentive to use it on a (6) ______ scale, production is bound to drop.

The Old House

The old house had sat (7) ______ for many years, its thatched roof slowly falling into disrepair. (8) ______ by tall trees, it was situated near a row of new semi-detached residences. Few noticed it sitting there all alone and forlorn.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.explanation: Đáp án chính thức: D - comes. Giải thích biên soạn/đề xuất: đặt đáp án vào chỗ trống rồi kiểm tra 3 lớp: collocation tự nhiên, sắc thái nghĩa, và cấu trúc ngữ pháp ngay trước/sau gap. Đáp án đúng là lựa chọn tạo cụm đúng tiếng Anh trong văn cảnh; nếu chỉ dịch từng từ hoặc chọn từ gần nghĩa nhưng không đi với cụm quanh gap thì sẽ sai. sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-15, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each space. Use only one word in each space.
sections.passageHtml:
Radio

The advent of television could have meant the end of the line for radio broadcasting, but over half a century later it is still going (9) ______. Every morning, listeners across the country tune (10) ______ to their favourite station as they wake up, drive to work or start going (11) ______ their daily chores. As social beings, we naturally (12) ______ out the company of others so, in situations where we are alone, the radio becomes the perfect surrogate companion.

As a result, in (13) ______ to watching television, (14) ______ to the radio tends to be more of a solitary pastime. So, what kind of entertainment is on (15) ______? Well, there is (16) ______ a wide range of broadcasts available that radio caters to almost all tastes: music, talk shows, documentaries, comedy — you name it, radio’s got it.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap in CAPITAL LETTERS.

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 16-25, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
Transatlantic Travel

At one time, for the majority of people needing to make the journey, the only way to travel from Europe to America was by ship, but the days of a leisurely 5-day (17) ______ (ATLANTIC) crossing in a large liner have long gone. Today, everything is about speed, and long-distance travel has become almost (18) ______ (EXCLUSIVE) the business of the airlines.

Although air travel is (19) ______ (ADMIT) fast, passengers are still subject to (20) ______ (EXPECT) hold-ups which seem to be inherent in any form of travel, but people’s reactions to such delays seem to be far more (21) ______ (PREDICT) than in the days of the great liners. Then, passengers might have shrugged their shoulders (22) ______ (RESIGN) and relaxed with a book in their cabins, knowing that little could be done about the situation, but today’s traveller is more likely to make his or her (23) ______ (SATISFY) known forcibly to any official who is (24) ______ (FORTUNE) enough to appear on the horizon.

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 32-39, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.
sections.passageHtml:
Key Word Transformation

For questions 25-30 (or 32-39), complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

questions.instruction: Nobody wants to buy second-hand computer equipment these days. (call) -> There is ____________________ second-hand computer equipment these days.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ALMOST NO CALL FOR (hoặc các biến thể chỉ lượng phủ định gần như hoàn toàn như virtually no / hardly any / scarcely any / very little / precious little). Giải thích: Cụm danh từ cố định 'call for something' = nhu cầu cho cái gì (demand for). 'Nobody wants to buy' tương đương với việc không có nhu cầu mua. Chuyên đề: Idioms & Collocations - call for.
questions.answerLocation: (almost/virtually) no OR hardly/scarcely any OR (very/precious) little call for

topic.title_vi: Viết lại câu với từ khóa
topic.path_en: CPE > Use of English > Key word transformation
topic.path_vi: CPE > Use of English > Viết lại câu với từ khóa

questions.instruction: This letter clearly says that you are entitled to attend the meeting. (right) -> This letter MAKES ____________________ attend the meeting.
acceptedAnswers: it clear that you have the right to
it clear you have the right to
it clear that you have every right to
it clear you have every right to
it clear that you have a right to
it clear you have a right to
it clear that it is your right to
it clear it is your right to
questions.correctAnswer: it clear that you have the right to
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IT CLEAR THAT YOU HAVE THE RIGHT TO (hoặc it clear you have every/a right to / it is your right to). Giải thích: 'Clearly says' đổi thành cụm động từ 'make it clear that...'. Khái niệm 'entitled to do something' (có quyền làm gì) đổi thành cụm 'have the right to do something' hoặc 'it is one's right to do something'. Chuyên đề: Fixed phrases - make it clear & have the right to.
questions.answerLocation: it clear (that) you have the/every/a right to OR it is your right to

topic.path_en: CPE > Use of English > Key word transformation
topic.path_vi: CPE > Use of English > Viết lại câu với từ khóa

questions.instruction: Martina was very annoyed that her son had borrowed her new bike. (great) -> To ____________________ borrowed her new bike.
acceptedAnswers: Martina's great annoyance, her son had
Martina's great annoyance her son had
questions.correctAnswer: Martina's great annoyance, her son had
questions.explanation: Đáp án: MARTINA'S GREAT ANNOYANCE, HER SON HAD. Giải thích: Cấu trúc trạng ngữ chỉ cảm xúc: 'To one's great + noun (annoyance/surprise/delight...)' = Trước sự vô cùng bực mình/ngạc nhiên của ai. Đổi tính từ 'very annoyed' thành cụm 'To Martina's great annoyance' và đảo vế quá khứ hoàn thành 'her son had'. Chuyên đề: Prepositional phrases showing emotion & tense consistency.
questions.answerLocation: Martina's great annoyance (1) + her son had (1)

questions.instruction: You should never leave this door unlocked under any circumstances. (circumstances) -> Under ____________________ left unlocked.
acceptedAnswers: no circumstances is this door to be
no circumstances is the door to be
no circumstances is this door ever to be
no circumstances is the door ever to be
questions.correctAnswer: no circumstances is this door to be
questions.explanation: Đáp án: NO CIRCUMSTANCES IS THIS DOOR TO BE (hoặc the door / ever to be). Giải thích: Đưa cụm trạng từ phủ định 'Under no circumstances' lên đầu câu yêu cầu đảo ngữ (Inversion): 'Under no circumstances + auxiliary verb + S + V'. Ở đây kết hợp cấu trúc bị động chỉ nghĩa nghĩa vụ: 'is this/the door (ever) to be left unlocked' (quy định không bao giờ được phép để cửa mở). Chuyên đề: Inversion (Đảo ngữ) & Modal structures.
questions.answerLocation: no circumstances (1) + is this/the door (ever) to be (1)

questions.instruction: There is every certainty that Joel will have finished the report by Monday. (bound) -> Joel ____________________ by Monday.
acceptedAnswers: is bound to have finished the report
is bound to have finished his report
is bound to have finished her report
questions.correctAnswer: is bound to have finished the report
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IS BOUND TO HAVE FINISHED THE REPORT. Giải thích: Cấu trúc cố định 'be bound to do something' = chắc chắn sẽ làm gì (chỉ sự tất yếu). Vì câu gốc dùng thì tương lai hoàn thành 'will have finished' để chỉ sự hoàn tất trước một thời điểm, ta biến đổi tương đương thành nguyên mẫu hoàn thành 'to have finished the/his/her report'. Chuyên đề: Modal adjectives - bound & Perfect infinitive.
questions.answerLocation: is bound (1) + to have finished the/his/her report (1)

questions.instruction: One day she’s going to become a famous film star. (matter) -> It's ____________________ a famous film star.
acceptedAnswers: a matter of time until she becomes
a matter of time before she becomes
a matter of time until she is
a matter of time before she is
questions.correctAnswer: a matter of time until she becomes
questions.explanation: Đáp án: A MATTER OF TIME UNTIL SHE BECOMES (hoặc before she becomes/is). Giải thích: Cấu trúc cố định 'It is a matter of time until/before...' = chỉ là vấn đề thời gian cho đến khi điều gì xảy ra. Do đó viết lại thành 'It's a matter of time until/before she becomes/is a famous star'. Chuyên đề: Fixed phrases - a matter of time.
questions.answerLocation: a matter of time (1) + until/before she becomes/is (1)

questions.instruction: Because of the appalling weather conditions, some trains will be delayed. (subject) -> Some trains ____________________ because of the appalling weather conditions.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ARE SUBJECT TO DELAY (hoặc will be subject to delays). Giải thích: Cấu trúc 'be subject to something' = có thể phải gánh chịu/bị ảnh hưởng bởi điều gì đó. Đổi động từ bị động 'will be delayed' thành cụm tính từ 'are/will be subject to' đi kèm danh từ 'delay' hoặc 'delays'. Chuyên đề: Adjective prepositions & Nominalisation.

topic.path_en: CPE > Use of English > Key word transformation
topic.path_vi: CPE > Use of English > Viết lại câu với từ khóa

questions.instruction: I don’t mind which make of car you choose. (consequence) -> It ____________________ make of car you choose.
acceptedAnswers: is of no consequence to me which
is of no consequence to me what
is of little consequence to me which
is of little consequence to me what
questions.correctAnswer: is of no consequence to me which
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IS OF NO CONSEQUENCE TO ME WHICH (hoặc of little consequence to me what). Giải thích: Cụm từ cố định 'be of no consequence (to someone)' = không quan trọng (đối với ai đó), đồng nghĩa với 'not mind'. Từ nghi vấn 'which make' có thể chấp nhận cả 'what make'. Chuyên đề: Idiomatic phrases - of no consequence & Synonyms.
questions.answerLocation: is of no/little consequence (1) + to me which/what (1)

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: For questions 19-40, read the texts below and choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the texts.
sections.passageHtml:
A Friend in Need

I really had no inkling of what was to ensue. We had always been very close of course; that went without saying. Being kept in the dark, therefore, about a matter of such consequence came not so much as a surprise but rather as a source of deep shock. It grieved me to have been hoodwinked, as it were, and at first my refusal to accept the situation clouded my better judgement. So I found myself in the uncomfortable position of being left in the cold and the coveted role of confidante denied me.

Our first encounter dates back a decade. I faintly recall a slender figure brushing past me on the first floor of a rather well-known department store, and as my glance was drawn to follow, my thoughts were interrupted, or perhaps even read: "Makes you sick, doesn’t it. I bet she only drinks water and feeds on morsels of fruit." Instinctively I turned to search for the owner of the voice. Directly behind me stood a woman of my age and my build. What my mother, bless her, had always encouraged in me as a healthy appetite had unfortunately turned to tubbiness in my middle years. "Makes you sick, I say." I suppose that I realised then that the speaker had been addressing me. "Fancy a cup of hot chocolate?" I offered shyly.

Our friendship blossomed. We found that we had a lot in common, and grew more inseparable as the months and years went by. At times our appearance drew the unwanted attention of others: awkward glances, sidelong looks, suppressed giggles. This was not unfamiliar either to me or to Geena I believe, but now the confidence found in the safety of numbers, albeit just two, drew me out of my shell and life took on a completely new outlook. We must have painted quite a picture the pair of us, two rather frumpish middle-aged ladies window-shopping in Oxford Street, making our way in and out of crowds of youngsters half our age. But we hadn't a care in the world; we were having the time of our lives.

However, neglect of one’s health takes its toll and two years ago I was diagnosed with a slight heart problem. Having been referred to a dietician I was faced with a strict regimen, and slowly but surely created a new lifestyle for myself. No longer did I take the bus just to pop into town, nor did I slump in front of the television of an evening, opting instead for a stroll in the local park. Supermarket shopping was the greatest strain; as I religiously avoided aisles previously worshipped. To my mild surprise, Geena seemed to take little interest in my health problem and it became a subject carefully avoided by the pair of us. I was not concerned however, as we continued spending a lot of time together and the ritual outings to the West End every other Saturday were devotedly adhered to. Only now I would order black coffee and toast instead of the former hot chocolate and Danish.

A more astute person, I am sure, would have picked up on the tell-tale signs but I have never been particularly discerning about other people’s feelings, having led a rather withdrawn life. Geena was in fact my sole companion, my mother having passed away years ago, and to be honest I had come to rely so much on her that I believe I was taking her for granted and consequently neglecting her own needs. In retrospect I realize that I was no longer a good friend and any attempt she may have made to communicate with me fell on deaf ears.

I heard from some that her wedding day was beautiful. I would have gone if it hadn't been for a previous arrangement I had made. She was very understanding of course, well that’s what had attracted me to her in the first place - her long-suffering patience and ability to understand. They say she made a lovely bride; apparently she even lost a bit of weight before the ceremony and looked a dream in her wedding outfit. At least I didn’t miss my doctor’s appointment: He’s rather concerned you see, as lately my blood pressure has gone up and I've put on some pounds again. But I’m sure it’s nothing to worry about. Perhaps I could phone Geena for a chat. That would cheer me up, and it’s four weeks now since she got back from her honeymoon. Well, maybe tomorrow. I don’t want to be a bother...

questions.instruction: Question 19: What does the writer acknowledge about his readership?
options: A. satisfy a number of people.
questions.explanation: Đáp án chính thức: A - satisfy a number of people. Giải thích biên soạn/đề xuất: đối chiếu option với evidence trong đoạn đọc. Đáp án đúng phải trả lời đúng trọng tâm câu hỏi, khớp phạm vi ý, thái độ người viết và quan hệ logic trong câu/đoạn. Nếu option chỉ lặp keyword nhưng đổi chủ thể, đổi mức độ, đảo nguyên nhân-kết quả hoặc thêm thông tin không có trong bài thì phải loại. Phân tích phương án sai: B (create the wrong impression.): distractor cần loại; hãy đối chiếu lại evidence để tìm lỗi thường gặp như sai phạm vi, không được nêu trực tiếp, đảo ý, hoặc chỉ trùng keyword.; C (be easy to prepare. :): distractor cần loại; hãy đối chiếu lại evidence để tìm lỗi thường gặp như sai phạm vi, không được nêu trực tiếp, đảo ý, hoặc chỉ trùng keyword.; D (be economical to use.): distractor cần loại; hãy đối chiếu lại evidence để tìm lỗi thường gặp như sai phạm vi, không được nêu trực tiếp, đảo ý, hoặc chỉ trùng keyword.
questions.answerLocation: A. satisfy a number of people.

questions.instruction: Question 21: One thing that occurred to Alistair about his office was that
options: A. he had forgotten to put away some important documents there.
B. he would have to do a lot of paperwork when he got back there.
C. he had nowhere to put the books and journals.
D. he could pretend to be surprised at its condition.
questions.explanation: Đáp án chính thức: D - he could pretend to be surprised at its condition. Giải thích biên soạn/đề xuất: đối chiếu option với evidence trong đoạn đọc. sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: For questions 37-43, read the text below. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap. There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Marchton Sport Club

It's a cold, frosty morning in Marchton and all over town men and women of all ages and backgrounds are rising bright and early, eagerly leaving behind their dream-filled slumber. No hitting the snooze button for them; no snuggling under warm blankets begging in muffled drowsiness for "just five more minutes ..." These are people filled with a sense of purpose, spurred on by a sense of loyalty to routine and in turn rewarded with a ready flow of endorphins.

[ Gap 37 ]

Specifically, I'm referring to the ninety members of the new fitness club situated in central Marchton. Not only are these participants given the opportunity to attend a wide variety of fitness classes or follow personal fitness programmes in state-of-the-art weight rooms, but they also have at their disposal two heated swimming pools and four squash courts, from which a choice can be made by even the most amateur of budding fitness hopefuls. So how do you join?

[ Gap 38 ]

However, as beneficial and tempting as all this may sound, it’s really no good if you have little or no staying power. Getting back to the current members, I quite simply posed the following question: how does one manage to get fit and then stay fit? What folk of mettle keep going and persevere throughout snowy January, drizzly April, and lazy August ...?

[ Gap 39 ]

It almost seems unfair, doesn’t it, such a decision first thing in the morning! We may be faced with that early morning state of drowsiness, but this is where the fight for fitness is either won or lost. It requires strength of mind long before you concern yourself with matters of the body. For beware! As you awake, a little voice suggests to you from your pillow that you don’t really have to get up early and go to the gym ... just five more minutes ...

[ Gap 40 ]

The people of the past, on the other hand, looked ahead. It is clear that they wanted their buildings to be there for future generations. This is corroborated by the fact that, in countries where the climate allows it, they planted trees. Think about it: planting a tree, especially one that will some day grow to be very big, is the ultimate in altruistic behaviour. When you plant an oak sapling, you know very well that you will not see the tree that it will become.

[ Gap 41 ]

There is a third element in today’s way of thinking that is particularly relevant to architecture, and that is the aesthetic element. This is a difficult area to deal with because it is extremely subjective. Beauty is, indeed, very often in the eye of the beholder; we all know what we like, and we all like different things. Even allowing for this, however, most people would probably agree that ‘beautiful’ is not the word they would use to describe the majority of modern buildings.

[ Gap 42 ]

Needless to say, however, there are a few instances when you should miss a workout and it’s very important to listen to what your body is telling you. It is also seriously unwise to take up any form of strenuous physical activity without first consulting your GP. Equally, you should provide your personal fitness trainer with details of your medical history so you can be advised accordingly on a suitable fitness programme.

[ Gap 43 ]

Whatever your age or health condition, however, the same message seems to be loud and clear; some form of physical activity is essential if we are to lead productive, fulfilling, stress-free lives.

questionGroups.instruction: Match the correct paragraph (A-H) to each gap.

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: I looked back into the courtyard, to where Father was now shining a flashlight down into the well and peering into the gloom. The drops I had left shone in the sunlight. I could not believe that nobody had seen them. Mother was screaming hysterically now; a sharp, jarring noise that I had never heard before. It shook my soul, suffused my conscience. What was I to do? I had had my revenge on you, but where did I go from here?

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: To this day I think I was almost getting away with it until Father appeared squatting in front of me. He had me repeat my story. I did so, hesitating, eyes narrowed.

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: It did not occur to me then that I had succeeded, in a sense, in my plan. What I saw were lights, strange, inchoate and bizarre. It was only later that I connected the visual symptoms of that fall and impact with the stylised stars and planets I was used to seeing drawn in a cartoon panel whenever a comic character suffered a similar whack.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: It was as though he was seeing me for the first time, and as though I was looking down a secret passage through time, to an adult perspective, to the way the world and cocky, lying children's stories would look to me when I was his age.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: That was what racked me, spread upon the castle's stones; that was what gripped me like a cold fist inside and squeezed those cold and bitter tears of grief from me and could not be comforted by Mother's soothing strokes and gentle pats and soft cooings.

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: The plan came to me like a lifebelt to a drowning man. I gathered all my courage and left my hiding place, coming staggering out and blinking. I cried out faintly, one hand to my brow, then yelled out a little louder when my first cry went unheeded. I stumbled on a little further, then collapsed dramatically on the cobbles.

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: I could hear raised, alarmed voices coming from the castle's main door. I ran the opposite way, down to the passage leading to the moat bridge, and hid in the shadows there.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: Arthur looked down at me, his expression regretful and troubled, shaking his head or looking like he wanted to, not because I had had a terrifying adventure and then been unjustly disbelieved by my own father, but because he too could see through my forlorn and hapless lie, and worried for the soul, the character, the future moral standing of any child so shameless - and so incompetent - in its too easily resorted-to lying.
